member.name: Rupert Lowe
member.synopsis: Rupert Lowe is the Restore Britain MP for Great Yarmouth , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Hate Crime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what plans she has for the retention, review and deletion of historic non-crime hate incident records following the replacement of the non-crime hate incident system.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the replacement system proposed by the College of Policing and National Police Chiefs’ Council in their joint review of non-crime hate incidents, hate-related non-criminal behaviour will be recorded only where there is a clear policing purpose, and ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Coastal Erosion: Property
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how many residential and commercial properties in England have been demolished, abandoned, or lost as a result of coastal erosion in each year since 2010, broken down by region and loc
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local Authorities have primary responsibility for managing coastal erosion; they are not required to report demolitions and may hold alternative records. The Environment Agency has a strategic overview of flooding and coastal erosion and administers the C...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Military Bases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what consideration is given to the needs of vulnerable individuals when placing asylum seekers in large-scale or military-site accommodation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Asylum seekers are allocated accommodation on a no choice basis, in line with Home Office published policy. If an individual’s needs cannot be met on site, and they are assessed as unsuitable to be allocated to or remain on site, they will be moved/alloca...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment has been made of the impact of geographic remoteness on the practical ability of asylum seekers to exercise freedom of movement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In accordance with current legislation, people with a valid pending claim for asylum are permitted to remain in the UK while their claim is being processed, and as they have permission to remain in the UK during that time, they cannot be routinely detaine...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Military Bases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether residents of asylum accommodation on Ministry of Defence sites are subject to any formal curfew or restrictions on hours of entry and exit.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In accordance with current legislation, people with a valid pending claim for asylum are permitted to remain in the UK while their claim is being processed, and as they have permission to remain in the UK during that time, they cannot be routinely detaine...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what internal discussions her Department has had on the publication of data on asylum accommodation sites and occupancy levels; and under what statutory, policy and civil service frameworks decisions
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office does not give a live commentary on the number of asylum seekers accommodated at accommodation sites.The Home Office publishes statistics on a quarterly basis detailing the number of supported asylum seekers accommodated in each local autho...

writtenQuestions.heading: Offences against Children: Greater Manchester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether her Department has received briefings on Operation Hexagon conducted by Greater Manchester Police since 2019.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Operation Hexagon was a police operation established by Greater Manchester Police in 2019 independently of Government.The Home Office is not aware of having received briefings on Operation Hexagon and currently holds only information that is publicly avai...

writtenQuestions.heading: Offences against Children: Greater Manchester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what information her Department holds on Operation Hexagon conducted by Greater Manchester Police.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Operation Hexagon was a police operation established by Greater Manchester Police in 2019 independently of Government.The Home Office is not aware of having received briefings on Operation Hexagon and currently holds only information that is publicly avai...

writtenQuestions.heading: Death Certificates

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has worked extensively with healthcare professionals and other stakeholders on the death certification reforms. The Department’s formal consultation in March 2016 sought direct views from National Health Service clinicians, medical examiner...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has worked extensively with healthcare professionals and other stakeholders on the death certification reforms. The Department’s formal consultation in March 2016 sought direct views from National Health Service clinicians, medical examiner...

writtenQuestions.heading: Death Certificates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what discussions his Department had with faith groups on the development of the death certification and registration reforms introduced in September 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has worked extensively with healthcare professionals and other stakeholders on the death certification reforms. The Department’s formal consultation in March 2016 sought direct views from National Health Service clinicians, medical examiner...

writtenQuestions.heading: Football: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what guidance her Department or its arm's length bodies, including Sport England, have issued to organisations such as County Football Associations on maintaining neutrality when delivering inclu
writtenQuestions.answerText: Neither Sport England nor the Football Foundation fund political campaigning or religious activity, as this is prohibited under National Lottery funding regulations. As such there is no definition of political and religious neutrality that applies to Coun...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.answerText: Neither Sport England nor the Football Foundation fund political campaigning or religious activity, as this is prohibited under National Lottery funding regulations. As such there is no definition of political and religious neutrality that applies to Coun...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Databases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, who is responsible for recording and reporting the number of asylum seekers at individual accommodation sites; and what systems are used to collect and manage that data.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Experienced specialists in asylum accommodation services, are appointed at sites. They are responsible for ensuring the safe and secure operation of the accommodation and for providing wraparound support that meets the essential health and wellbeing needs...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Crowborough Training Camp
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what (a) procedures and (b) mechanisms are in place to control entry and exit at the asylum accommodation site in Crowborough; and what guidance is issued to security staff on how to verify and differ
writtenQuestions.answerText: Clearsprings Ready Homes, an experienced specialist in asylum accommodation services, has been appointed by the Home Office to manage the site. They are responsible for ensuring the safe and secure operation of the accommodation and for providing wraparou...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Crowborough Training Camp
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what proportion of individuals accommodated at the asylum site in Crowborough at its opening remain in residence; and what proportion have subsequently left, including through relocation, grant of sta
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office does not give a live commentary on the number of asylum seekers accommodated at accommodation sites.

writtenQuestions.heading: Memorials: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what funding has been provided to support war memorials dedicated to specific faith groups in the last ten years, broken down by religion.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government funding for the design and construction of war memorials, including those associated with particular faith groups, is considered on a case-by-case basis. In May 2026, the Government announced up to £970,000 of funding towards the creation of a ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Migrant Workers: Visas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, how many applications for overseas workers hid Department received under the Visa Fees Reimbursement Scheme for Scale-Ups, broken down by nationality, in each year since it has been in operation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Business and Trade launched the Visa Fees Reimbursement Scheme for Scale-Ups on 10 June as part of the Government's Modern Industrial Strategy. The scheme - worth up to £25K for around 50 businesses a year - supports eligible scale-ups ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pupils: Asylum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how many children of asylum seekers are enrolled in state-funded (a) primary and (b) secondary schools; and whether such data is available by region and local authority.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Education does not hold data on pupils at state-funded schools in England who are the children of asylum seekers.

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Fraud
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps his Department is taking to tackle fraud involving disability benefit claimants undertaking undeclared work; and how many such cases have been identified in the last 12 months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Where a concern is raised about an individual’s entitlement to benefit, whether through a public referral or through other intelligence, the department will always take appropriate action. Benefit fraud occurs where an individual dishonestly makes a decla...
